command: $gid=$(az ad group show --group "Marketing Team" --query id -o tsv); $uid=$(az ad user show --id "jordan.hall@contosomktg.onmicrosoft.com" --query id -o tsv); az ad group member add --group $gid --member-id $uid; az ad group member list --group $gid --query "[?id=='$uid']" -o json
explanation: Adds the persona to a benign-looking group so their presence and access patterns appear normal.
rationale_steps: Resolve group and user IDs using Azure CLI.
Add the user to a common team group to blend with expected access.
List membership and filter to confirm in JSON format.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell variable assignments and semicolon separators are valid; quoting is balanced and az CLI flags/queries are well-formed.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command uses Azure CLI to fetch IDs, add the user to a group, and verify membership—consistent with abusing accounts to blend into normal activity—though it requires appropriate directory/group privileges despite the 'None' claim.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is mostly well-formed PowerShell using valid Azure CLI syntax with proper variable assignment and chaining via semicolons, though mixing bash-style `$()` subshell substitution with PowerShell variable assignment (`$gid=$(...)`) works in PowerShell but is slightly unconventional and could cause issues depending on the PowerShell version.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1585 by adding a created persona to an existing legitimate-looking group to normalize their presence and access patterns, using appropriate Azure CLI commands (`az ad group member add`) with correct argument structure to blend the account into normal organizational activity.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a perfectly well-formed PowerShell sequence using valid subexpression syntax, variable assignments, and command separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command logic perfectly matches the intent of the technique by manipulating group membership to blend in; however, 'az ad group show' requires the '--id' flag rather than '--group' to identify the target group, which would cause the first part of the command to fail in current Azure CLI versions.
